fields.description:
# Workflow-engine remediation: unify LLM functionality on the engine, restore visual editing, document intended use

## Context

Captures the remediation-on-top-of-the-engine scope that surfaced finalizing the
plan-review gate. The original long-form scope-capture prose (workstream catalog +
research notes) is preserved in this ticket's EDIT HISTORY and in the re-triage
COMMENTS below; this body is the current, executable state. Related: engine epic
`hump-seam-spice` (e6d9, closed), gate epic `messy-moose-jig` (5fd2, closed),
three-pass epic `sheer-myth-idol` (9da1).

## Current scope & disposition (2026-06-27)

The historical workstreams are RESOLVED; this is the authoritative current state:

- DONE via closed sibling epics — WS-INTENT-ALIGN, WS-MIGRATE, WS-PROMPTS, WS-CLEANUP,
  WS-VISUAL, WS-VALIDATE: delivered by epic A `sage-votary-logic` (5766), epic B
  `peak-fir-hilt` (72d5), and engine epic e6d9. The earlier open-ended decision
  investigations (DSL map/fan-out, exact cleanup set, visual-format choice) were
  settled there — they are no longer open questions in this epic.
- SPLIT OUT — WS-VISUAL-EVAL is moved to its own epic `yip-gem-teak` (7013); OUT OF
  SCOPE here.
- REMAINING (this epic's only live deliverables) — WS-DOC and WS-EVAL-EXISTING,
  decomposed into child stories `seek-ditch-corgi` (WS-DOC) and `rink-cloud-gum`
  (WS-EVAL-EXISTING; task children: scorer registry + dataset schema, the 3 specs,
  the live solver + fixtures, and the run_eval/CI wiring).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/workflow-engine.md` exists, is linked from README and the CLAUDE.md doc index, and separates a CLIENT (run/use) audience from a REBAR-DEV (author/edit) audience.
- [ ] `docs/workflow-engine.md` documents the YAML DSL, scripted-vs-agentic steps, the agent-vs-single-turn bright line, the three-pass review pattern, and the eval seam (two-tier model, how the live tier is triggered, cost + fork-safety rails); it cross-links rather than duplicates docs/llm-framework.md and docs/plan-review-gate.md.
- [ ] Eval specs exist for `completion-verifier`, `ticket-quality`, and `spec-alignment` under `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/`, each passing `validate_eval_spec` with a cross-family judge (gates:false), `at_least(k)` over explicit epochs, a coverage threshold, and a non-empty gold_set.
- [ ] A deterministic-scorer registry exists; every scorer name used by the packaged specs maps to an implemented pure function, and `validate_scorer` rejects an unregistered name.
- [ ] `run_eval()` runs a real live eval (no longer raises NotImplementedError) via a native loop over the eval solver (Inspect AI intentionally not used — agentic ops don't fit its single-completion model; rationale in eval.py); the three store/repo-coupled agentic reviewers run against a disposable fixture store via the solver.
- [ ] `eval-discipline` CI validates all packaged specs on every PR with no model call; offline unit tests assert dataset balance and a non-empty gold_set.
- [ ] `eval-live` CI runs on workflow_dispatch plus a weekly schedule, is non-blocking (continue-on-error), response-cached, concurrency-capped, and fork-safe (no live call on fork PRs or Dependabot); provider spend-cap guidance is documented.
- [ ] Every child ticket of this epic is closed (each signed by the completion-verifier gate).

## Success Criteria

- [ ] A new contributor can read `docs/workflow-engine.md` and correctly decide when to author a workflow vs a bespoke op, and how to run and trigger evals, without reading source.
- [ ] A prompt edit to any packaged reviewer prompt is regression-gated by an eval that actually scores the output, not one that only shape-validates the spec.
